stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • When law students discuss real cases publicly online, they may violate attorney-client privilege.
  • Hannah Park led a study to determine how many law schools have student policies that address online discussion of cases.
  • Park and her team reviewed 178 law school websites, examining publicly available student handbooks.
  • Only twenty-three law schools had guidelines that explicitly mention online case discussion, and only nine defined what constitutes acceptable conduct.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the study’s methodology. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The student handbooks of 178 law schools can be found online, according to research by Hannah Park.
Out of 178 law schools, only twenty-three had student policies that mentioned online case discussion, and only nine specified what conduct was acceptable.
Hannah Park and her team wanted to know how many law schools have student policies in place about protecting attorney-client privilege online.
To find out how many law schools have guidelines about online case discussion, Hannah Park and her team examined the student handbooks of 178 law schools.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The sentence effectively emphasizes Park’s methodology: examining the student handbooks of 178 law schools for guidelines about online case discussion.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence specifies how many law schools’ handbooks are available online; it doesn’t emphasize the study’s methodology. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes the study’s results, not the methodology. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes the aim of the study, not the methodology.
